summary: Jinneng raised PP yarn/inj 20 USD to 1,010-1,020 USD/ton FOB
Lotte Hyundai PP yarn closed lowest at 960 USD/ton CIF (down 30)
SE Asia PP fell 40 USD to 1,005 CFR while NE Asia rose 15 (Platts)
paras: Between last week and now, Jinneng's distributor reported PP yarn/injection offering prices from Jinneng under FOB Qingdao (China) conditions to Vietnamese buyers at USD 990/ton; BOPP offer at USD 1,000/ton FOB, delivery July 2026. Today, 9/7, the export PP yarn/injection and BOPP offering prices from Jinneng to Vietnam increased USD 20/ton from the previous week, to USD 1,010–1,020/ton FOB. Delivery August 7–8/2026.

Besides Jinneng, from yesterday, PP/BOPP offering prices from Petrochina, and PP Fibre offering prices from Sinopec Trunk China were also adjusted upward by USD 20–25/ton compared to the beginning of the week, with the prevailing PP yarn/injection offering price at USD 1,030–1,040/ton CIF. Prices are rising amid increasing raw material costs following renewed US–Iran tensions.

However, the closing PP yarn sale price from Lotte Hyundai South Korea for Vietnamese buyers as of today recorded the lowest level at USD 960/ton CIF, more than USD 30/ton lower than the closing price at the beginning of this week. The closing price for PP yarn Y130 from Lotte South Korea was also recorded at USD 990/ton CIF. The distributor's closing at low prices may have been done earlier to clear a certain volume of goods.

Domestically, the price decline of PP is slower, with less volatility compared to PE, but there are no clear signs that prices could rise again. A slight positive signal is that as import offering prices increased this week, the psychological effect has prompted some buyers to quickly finalize import orders at current low offering prices, which can help suppliers clear inventory faster.

Specifically for PP yarn today, the main domestic products remain from NSRP and BSR. In this market, the PP yarn price T3034 from BSR dropped further to VND 29,800/kg incl. VAT. In the northern market, transactions for PP 114G from NSRP were recorded at VND 29,500/kg incl. VAT, while many other traders offered prices ranging from VND 30,000–30,500/kg incl. VAT.

Sibur Russia's distributor has not shipped PP to Vietnam for the past several weeks, while PP from China has become quite cheap. PP copo offering prices also saw some sellers adjusting down an additional VND 300–500/kg. In the near/off segment, competitive pressure is also very strong. A northern seller has lowered the PP random off offering price from Japan to VND 32,500/kg incl. VAT, down as much as VND 1,500/kg from their prices at the beginning of the week.

Regionally, according to Platts' assessment published today (9/7), in Northeast Asia, PP yarn/injection prices this week increased USD 15/ton compared to the previous week, while BOPP dropped USD 20/ton and PP block copo dropped USD 10/ton. In Southeast Asia, prices of all PP types this week continued to decline sharply by USD 40/ton from the previous week; with PP yarn/injection dropping to USD 1,005/ton CFR, BOPP dropping to USD 1,025/ton CFR, IPP dropping to USD 1,055/ton CFR, and block copolymer dropping to USD 1,005/ton CFR.

PP prices in Asia showed mixed trends during the week as markets in China and Southeast Asia reacted differently to rising Brent crude oil futures on July 8. China's domestic market responded quickly to the strong increase in oil prices, despite the market's fundamentals remaining stable. The increase in PP prices in China was accompanied by a surge in prices of all other petrochemical products. Meanwhile in Southeast Asia, especially Vietnam, PP continued to decline as South Korean and other suppliers lowered offering prices to meet the prices that Vietnamese buyers were offering, amid oversupply conditions.

summary: Lotte Titan cut LDPE 370 and HDPE blow 220 USD/ton month-on-month
SE Asia HDPE fell 100 USD to 1,050-1,060 USD/ton CFR (Platts)
South LLDPE offers down 1,500-2,000 to 28,800-29,800 VND/kg
paras: Today (9/7), the PE plastic resin market in Vietnam continues its sideways-to-downward trend, despite US–Iran tensions in the Middle East heating up and the Chinese plastic market rising. The reasons are abundant supply sources, competitive offering prices, and limited demand.

Specifically, in the south, LLDPE Film/injection offering prices from various origins fluctuated in the common range of VND 28,800–29,800/kg incl. VAT, declining by as much as VND 1,500–2,000/kg from the previous week. In the north, some high-quality LLDPE Film grades from Sabic, Qamar of Saudi Arabia such as 218WJ, FD21HS have been recorded with buyers accepting the lowest purchase price at VND 29,500/kg incl. VAT, down VND 700–1,000/kg from the lowest selling price last weekend.

For LDPE, the offering price level for various LDPE Film grades in the south today has fallen to VND 33,000–38,000/kg incl. VAT; of which US-origin LDPE Film has offering prices on the lower end around VND 33,000–35,000/kg incl. VAT, while LDPE Film from the Middle East and Southeast Asia (such as 4025AS, 260GG, 2426K) has offering prices in the range of VND 36,000–38,000/kg incl. VAT. In the north, sellers are maintaining higher offering prices, around VND 36,000–39,000/kg incl. VAT.

For HDPE, grades HDPE F1 from Qamar, TR144 from Marlex, and FJ00952 from Sabic of Saudi Arabia are widely offered at VND 35,500–36,000/kg incl. VAT; HDPE blow prices are also declining more sharply. LSP's distributor is offering HDPE blow 5502 from CP Chem Singapore at VND 31,500/kg incl. VAT; some other traders have lowered HDPE blow offering prices from China and the US to VND 29,000–30,000/kg incl. VAT.

Earlier in the week, at Hai Phong port, Sibur Russia's distributor offered HDPE Film at VND 31,000/kg incl. VAT, HDPE Film off grade at VND 30,000/kg incl. VAT, HDPE blow off grade at VND 27,600/kg incl. VAT, LLDPE Film at VND 28,500–29,000/kg incl. VAT depending on grade, LLDPE C6 off grade at VND 28,500/kg incl. VAT, and mLLDPE at VND 31,200/kg incl. VAT.

Notably, Sibur's distributor revealed that after the US conducted airstrikes on Iran and tensions between the two countries escalated during the week, the number of Vietnamese customers inquiring and making spot purchases increased significantly. Sibur is prioritizing shifting goods to sell to China, amid more active futures trading in the Chinese market this week, with rising prices.

Regarding imports, today (9/7), Lotte Titan Malaysia has offered PE and PP import prices for August delivery to Vietnam. Prices were adjusted very sharply down from the previous month. Accordingly, LDPE offering prices of all types from Titan this month dropped by a total of USD 370/ton compared to the previous month's offerings. Specifically, LDPE Film prices for common grades 260GG/200GG at USD 1,130/ton CIF, LLDPE injection 300YY and LDPE coating 800YY at USD 1,180–1,190/ton CIF. HDPE blow offering prices from Titan dropped USD 220/ton from the previous month, to USD 1,100/ton CIF. HDPE Film offering prices dropped USD 200/ton to USD 1,080/ton CIF.

Also today, Lotte South Korea's distributor offered HDPE Film FL7000 and HDPE yarn MF5000 for import to Vietnam at USD 1,000/ton CIF, delivery August 2026. According to sources, this offer matches the closing price from the previous half-month (the July 2026 lot was offered at USD 1,070/ton CIF but closed at USD 1,000/ton CIF). At this price, HDPE from South Korea is becoming more attractive than offerings from China.

Earlier, the LDPE Film closing sale price from Lotte Hyundai South Korea for Vietnamese buyers was announced at USD 1,070/ton CIF, more than USD 30/ton lower than the offer price released by the supplier earlier today. PTT Thailand's distributor this week also offered LLDPE Film to Vietnam at USD 940–960/ton CIF. Meanwhile, HDPE Film offering prices for Vietnam were at USD 980–1,000/ton CIF depending on brand and quantity. This price was flat to down USD 20/ton compared to the previous week (the previous week's lowest closing price was USD 1,000/ton).

Equate Kuwait this week offered LLDPE Film 7050 to Vietnam at USD 950/ton CIF; HDPE Film 6888 at USD 1,000/ton CIF. For Chinese-origin goods, LLDPE Film 7042N/7047 from Petrochina at USD 940–950/ton CIF; HDPE Film FS7000 from STL increased slightly to USD 1,080–1,090/ton CIF.

Regionally, according to Platts' price assessment, in Northeast Asia, prices of all HDPE Film/yarn/blow/injection types this week declined USD 10/ton compared to the previous week, LLDPE dropped USD 70/ton, and LDPE dropped USD 50/ton. In Southeast Asia, prices of all HDPE types this week also dropped sharply by USD 100/ton from the previous week, with HDPE falling to USD 1,050–1,060/ton CFR; LLDPE dropping sharply by USD 140/ton to USD 1,010/ton CFR; LLDPE metallocene C6 dropping USD 140/ton to USD 1,160/ton CFR; and LDPE dropping USD 100/ton to USD 1,350/ton CFR.

Despite the declining price level, the PE market in Asia has fallen into a state of uncertainty due to the absence of trade negotiations, especially after Middle East conflicts escalated again and PE futures contract prices in China rose in line with crude oil. Market participants believe that trading activity in the short term could remain limited due to the uncertainty over market trends.

summary: Yihua SG-5 north cut 400 to 20,000 VND/kg; Zhongtai at 19,910
Taiyo TH1000 offered 650 USD/ton CIF (down ~100 month-on-month)
China PVC stable at 745 USD/ton CFR (Platts); futures firmer
paras: This week, domestic PVC offering prices are witnessing traders aggressively cutting prices to compete, amid sluggish demand as the market enters the rainy season – the traditional mid-year low point. Many sellers are even accepting losses on bulk orders imported earlier at high prices, before the price floor drops further.

Today, 9/7, in the north, the main distributor of PVC SG-5 (coal-based) from Yihua China has lowered the offering price to VND 20,000/kg incl. VAT at Yen Vien station (Hanoi), down VND 400/kg from the beginning of the week. Another distributor offering PVC SG-5 from Zhongtai China at VND 19,910/kg incl. VAT – officially breaking below the "floor" of VND 20,000/kg incl. VAT that had held since early March 2026.

Despite the price decline, transaction volumes remain sluggish. The reason is that coal-based PVC is still seeing even sharper price reductions, creating intense competitive pressure. One major distributor, Stavian, has lowered the offering price for PVC SE950 from Shintech USA for large northern customers to VND 20,200/kg incl. VAT, only VND 200–300/kg higher than coal-based PVC from China.

Similarly in the south, some coal-based PVC SG-5 from China has been offered at VND 20,000/kg incl. VAT – matching the northern offering price. However, many customers are bidding at VND 19,500/kg incl. VAT. Also in the south, a trader has lowered the offering price for PVC PM66R from domestic manufacturer AGC Vina (ACVN) to VND 20,300/kg incl. VAT, down VND 600/kg from the beginning of the week and lower than the factory's announced price for June.

Regarding imports, today (9/7), the commercial distributor of Taiyo Japan's PVC TH1000 is offering to the Vietnamese market this month at USD 650/ton CIF, with cargo volume of approximately 1,500–2,000 tons. This price has dropped about USD 100/ton compared to the beginning of the previous month (the previous month had no widespread offers but transaction prices were around USD 750/ton CIF).

A distributor is offering PVC S1000 (oil-based) from Huayi China to Vietnam at USD 640/ton CIF HCMC, also sharply down compared to the same period last month (oil-based PVC offering prices from China in early June were at USD 710–730/ton CIF).

A commercial distributor is offering PVC grade 266RC from Vinylthai Thailand (under AGC Groups – AVT) to Vietnam this week at USD 660/ton CFR. This may be leftover stock that does not yet reflect AVT's new monthly offering price. Previously, AVT offered PVC to Vietnam at USD 750–760/ton CIF, so this week's offering has dropped by USD 100–110/ton.

Regionally, according to Platts' price assessment published today, the PVC price level in China this week remains stable at USD 745/ton CFR; PVC prices in Southeast Asia dropped USD 5/ton from the previous week to USD 695/ton CFR. Notably, oil-based PVC selling prices in mainland China increased following new developments at the Strait of Hormuz, and customer purchasing volumes also rose. However, many market participants believe the fundamental factors of the PVC market have not changed significantly.

PP.sub: Mixed: China lifts, SE Asia falls
